new_vs_pre_existing_mechanism.description: Every finding object carries an explicit boolean `introduced_in_diff`. Findings with `introduced_in_diff: true` were newly created by this cycle's diff. Findings with `introduced_in_diff: false` are pre-existing repo state surfaced as advisory context (default-safe: close.md treats these as non-blocking per AC-2.6 (b) default-safe ignore rule). The orchestrator-supplied changed-files list was used to scope the audit; all findings below reference files that appear in (or are direct consequences of) that list.

non_functional_files.reason: 0-byte runtime flock lock file created by scripts/score-update.sh (line 58: LOCK_FILE="${SCORES_FILE}.lock"). Persists in working tree at project root after every score-update invocation. NOT covered by .gitignore — currently shows up as untracked. Runtime artifact, never has semantic content, must not be tracked.
non_functional_files.proposed_fix: Append exact-path rule to .gitignore: `/agent-scores.json.lock` (do NOT use blanket `*.lock` — conventionally tracked locks like poetry.lock, Cargo.lock, package-lock.json must remain trackable in other projects sharing this .gitignore lineage).
non_functional_files.blocks_close_reason: file-level finding in --changed-files mode with explicit positive NEW marker; per AC-2.6 (b) NEW-positive marker rule this is a legitimate cleanliness blocker — but severity major and per default-safe rules orchestrator may treat as non-blocking advisory if close.md disposition policy chooses.

non_functional_files.reason: Mutable runtime scoring state. The file contains per-agent score history with absolute ISO-8601 timestamps and per-task notes (e.g., line 11 `"ts": "2026-05-19T17:15:08Z"`, line 16 `"note": "20260519-160856"`). This is not a schema bootstrap — it is live telemetry that changes on every /close cycle. Currently NOT in .gitignore and NOT in git index. If a future commit accidentally `git add`s it, every cycle thereafter produces a noisy diff and merge conflicts across machines.
non_functional_files.proposed_fix: Append `/agent-scores.json` to .gitignore. If a seed/schema-bootstrap copy is desired for new installations, track a separate `agent-scores.example.json` (or have score-update.sh seed defaults on first run from an embedded heredoc — which it already does per line 50: 'Ensure scores file exists; if not, create empty schema').
non_functional_files.blocks_close_reason: file-level finding with explicit positive NEW marker. Functional value: file is live state — must NOT be deleted, only gitignored.

gitignore_gaps.reason: Existing .gitignore line 75 has `specs/spec-*/` and line 71 has `specs/*/cp-state-*.json` — but neither pattern matches `.claude/specs/...`. Result: 21 cp-state-*.json files + 22 cp-state-*.json.lock files + 1 .cp-checkin.lock under `.claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/` are all unmatched by current rules. They are runtime spec-checkpoint state, not source artifacts. This cycle is the FIRST cycle to populate `.claude/specs/`, so this is a NEW gitignore gap introduced by this diff's spec-checkpoint workflow.
gitignore_gaps.proposed_fix: Add to .gitignore: `/.claude/specs/spec-*/` (broad, matches the convention already used for `specs/spec-*/` on line 75). Alternative narrower form: `/.claude/specs/*/cp-state-*.json`, `/.claude/specs/*/cp-state-*.json.lock`, `/.claude/specs/*/.cp-checkin.lock`.
gitignore_gaps.blocks_close_reason: file-level finding with explicit positive NEW marker. Advisory severity major — close.md can choose to treat as non-blocking if cycle policy allows.

gitignore_gaps.reason: agents/test-writer.md (line 39, 41, 80) and downstream consumers in commands/dev.md and settings.json reference `tests/generated/<task_id>/test_AC<NN>_<ac_uid>.py` and `tests/generated/<task_id>/manifest.json` as future generated artifacts. The directory does NOT exist yet in the working tree, so this is preventative. If these are intended to be committed regression tests, ignore this finding. If they are runtime artifacts (manifest.json with hashes/timestamps), they should be gitignored before the first /dev cycle that produces them.
gitignore_gaps.proposed_fix: Resolve policy: (a) if committed regression tests — no change needed; (b) if runtime artifacts — add `/tests/generated/manifest.json` and consider whether the per-task `test_AC*.py` skeletons should be committed.
gitignore_gaps.blocks_close_reason: Advisory; no concrete violation yet because the directory does not exist.

doc_index_pollution.reason: Auto-generated doc index at README.md:16-17 now lists `agent-scores.json` and `agent-scores.json.lock` as project files. These entries should disappear once those files are gitignored; if the index generator scans the working tree (not the git index), regenerate after the .gitignore fix. INDEX.md:3399-3400 has the same pollution.
doc_index_pollution.proposed_fix: After adding the two gitignore entries above (agent-scores.json and agent-scores.json.lock), regenerate README.md and INDEX.md so the polluting entries are removed. If the index generator is git-index-driven this happens automatically once the files are no longer tracked; if it is working-tree-driven, it should be patched to skip gitignored paths.
doc_index_pollution.blocks_close_reason: Cosmetic — index pollution is a downstream effect of the two real findings above; resolves automatically when those are fixed.

doc_index_pollution.reason: Same as README.md — lines 3399-3400 list `agent-scores.json` and `agent-scores.json.lock`. Will resolve once gitignore is fixed and index is regenerated.

new_scripts.evidence: 12KB, executable bit set, well-documented module docstring (lines 1-20), referenced from 5+ files (agents/qa.md, commands/dev.md, agents/ba.md, scripts/README.md, scripts/INDEX.md).
6.4KB, executable, referenced from commands/close.md and scripts indexes.
3.5KB, executable, referenced from commands/dev.md and commands/close.md.
6.1KB, executable, has usage banner + exit-code documentation + flock locking, referenced from commands/close.md and scripts indexes. Lock file produced is the gitignore gap above.

new_agents.evidence: 9.1KB. Referenced by agents/qa.md, commands/dev.md, agents/ba.md, and 5 cp-state files. Not on the orphan-agents exception list — but it is referenced, so it would not be flagged by detect-orphan-agents.sh anyway.

new_docs.evidence: Lives under `docs/dev/specs/` which is correct location for /spec output. Parent `docs/` is gitignored (.gitignore line 66), so this doc never enters the index — no doc-placement violation.

modified_files.evidence: No misplaced content, no stale references; references new scripts/agents that were also added in this cycle.
Same — content updates align with new harness components.
Hook/permission updates that pair with new scripts; no orphaned blocks observed in the changed lines for this cycle.

summary.estimated_space_saved: negligible (0-byte lock file; agent-scores.json ~3.5KB; cp-state directory grows continuously if tracked)
summary.blocks_close_explanation: All findings are file-level. In --changed-files mode with mechanism (ii) tagging, every finding carries explicit `introduced_in_diff: true`, so close.md MAY treat the 3 major findings as new-blocking per AC-2.6 (b) NEW-positive marker rule. However, all three are .gitignore hygiene issues, not in-source dirt, and have concrete one-line fixes — recommended disposition is non-blocking advisory with a follow-up cleanup commit after this cycle closes.
summary.primary_action_recommended: Append three lines to .gitignore: `/agent-scores.json`, `/agent-scores.json.lock`, `/.claude/specs/spec-*/`. Then regenerate README.md / INDEX.md / .claude/INDEX.md so the polluting entries vanish.

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex (gpt-5.5, xhigh) materially expanded the draft by identifying two findings I had missed: (a) the entire .claude/specs/<spec-id>/ cp-state tree is NOT covered by the existing `specs/spec-*/` gitignore rule — 21 cp-state-*.json files, 22 .lock files, and .cp-checkin.lock are all leaking into git-status; (b) the auto-generated README.md / INDEX.md doc indexes are polluted with the unignored runtime entries. Codex also upgraded my draft severities (agent-scores.json from observation_only → major, agent-scores.json.lock from minor → major), confirmed `*.lock` is too broad (would catch poetry.lock / Cargo.lock), and flagged tests/generated/ as a future policy decision (observation_only).

findings.issue: agent-scores.json.lock should be major severity, not minor; and `*.lock` blanket pattern is too broad (would hit poetry.lock, Cargo.lock, package-lock.json in other projects).
findings.proposed_fix: Use exact `/agent-scores.json.lock` rule; raise severity to major.
findings.rationale: Codex is correct on both points: severity raised, blanket pattern rejected in favor of exact rule. Both fixes incorporated into the report.

findings.issue: agent-scores.json should be major (not observation_only) — it's live telemetry with timestamps, not schema bootstrap.
findings.proposed_fix: Add `/agent-scores.json` to .gitignore; if seed needed, track agent-scores.example.json.
findings.rationale: Codex is correct — the file's `history[]` entries are mutable per-cycle telemetry, not a static schema. Upgraded severity and adopted the example-file alternative as the recommended fix.

findings.issue: .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/ cp-state files are NOT covered by the existing `specs/spec-*/` gitignore rule because that rule lacks the .claude/ prefix.
findings.rationale: Verified by running `git check-ignore .claude/specs/spec-20260518-225715/cp-state-dev.json` — exit 1 (not ignored). The existing line 75 `specs/spec-*/` rule does NOT match `.claude/specs/...`. New finding added to gitignore_gaps section. This is the most consequential omission from my draft.

findings.issue: README.md / INDEX.md auto-generated doc indexes are polluted with runtime artifact entries (agent-scores.json, agent-scores.json.lock).
findings.proposed_fix: Regenerate doc indexes after .gitignore is updated; if generator is working-tree-driven, patch it to skip gitignored paths.
findings.rationale: Verified at README.md:16-17 and INDEX.md:3399-3400. Added to new doc_index_pollution finding category. Severity minor because it is a downstream cosmetic effect that resolves automatically once the .gitignore fixes are made.

findings.issue: tests/generated/ policy decision — could be committed regression tests OR runtime artifacts.
findings.proposed_fix: Determine policy: if runtime, add `/tests/generated/manifest.json` to .gitignore.
findings.rationale: Verified that `tests/generated/` does not yet exist in the working tree, so no concrete violation. Logged as advisory under gitignore_gaps with severity minor. Will become actionable on the first /dev cycle that runs test-writer.

findings.issue: Confirmation that draft was otherwise complete (scripts placed correctly, executable bits set, modified .md files clean, no missed orphans).
findings.rationale: Codex agreement with the rest of the draft — recorded under verified_clean to acknowledge.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: Two severities raised (lock file minor→major, scores.json observation_only→major). Two new findings added: (1) .claude/specs/spec-*/ gitignore gap covering 44 unignored runtime files, (2) README.md/INDEX.md doc-index pollution. One new finding category (doc_index_pollution) introduced. Recommended fix list expanded from 1 gitignore line to 3 gitignore lines plus an index regeneration step. tests/generated/ logged as observation_only pending policy decision.
